# KubeNSync

> Kubernetes operator that automatically creates and synchronizes resources across namespaces using Go templates.

You define a `ManagedResource` (cluster-scoped CRD) with a namespace selector and a Go template. The operator renders the template once per matching namespace and creates, updates, or deletes the resulting Kubernetes resources. It tracks everything it creates in `status.createdResources` and cleans up when a namespace stops matching.

## Key facts

- Single CRD: `ManagedResource` (API group `automation.kubensync.com/v1alpha1`, short name `mr`, cluster-scoped)
- Template engine: Go `text/template` + Sprout function library
- Template context: `.Namespace` (full `corev1.Namespace` object) and `.Data` (map of external data sources)
- Multiple resources per template separated by `---`
- `avoidResourceUpdate: true` prevents updates to existing resources (create-only mode)
- Namespace selector: Go regex AND/OR Kubernetes label selector (both must match if both are set)

## Template data sources

Three types can be injected into `.Data` via `spec.template.data[]`. They differ in how values are accessed inside the template:

**`Secret`** — `ref` requires only `name` and `namespace`. Produces a flat key/value map. Values are automatically base64-decoded (Kubernetes stores secret data as base64; the operator decodes it before injection). Re-encode with `base64Encode` when the destination field expects base64.
```
.Data.<name>.<key>  →  string (decoded)
```
```yaml
# ref
- name: my_secret
  type: Secret
  ref:
    name: my-secret
    namespace: default
# usage — index is required for keys containing dots or special characters
data:
  .dockerconfigjson: '{{ index .Data.my_secret ".dockerconfigjson" | base64Encode }}'
  token: '{{ .Data.my_secret.token }}'
```

**`ConfigMap`** — `ref` requires only `name` and `namespace`. Produces a flat key/value map. Values are plain strings, no transformation applied.
```
.Data.<name>.<key>  →  string
```
```yaml
# ref
- name: my_cm
  type: ConfigMap
  ref:
    name: my-configmap
    namespace: default
# usage
data:
  config: '{{ .Data.my_cm.some_key }}'
```

**`KubernetesResource`** — `ref` requires `name`, `namespace`, `kind`, `group` (empty string for core resources), and `apiVersion` (version only, e.g. `v1`, `v1beta1`). The full raw Kubernetes object is unmarshaled into a nested map — no key decoding or transformation. Access any field by its path in the object.
```
.Data.<name>.<field>.<subfield>  →  any
```
```yaml
# ref
- name: my_sa
  type: KubernetesResource
  ref:
    apiVersion: v1
    group: ""
    kind: ServiceAccount
    name: my-service-account
    namespace: default
# usage
data:
  serviceAccountName: '{{ .Data.my_sa.metadata.name }}'
  uid: '{{ .Data.my_sa.metadata.uid }}'
```

## Template functions

The template engine provides Go's built-in functions plus the full [Sprout](https://github.com/go-sprout/sprout) library.

**Go built-ins:** `len`, `index`, `slice`, `printf`, `range`, `if`, `with`, `and`, `or`, `not`

**Sprout highlights:**

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `base64Encode` | Encode string to base64 |
| `base64Decode` | Decode base64 string |
| `toYAML` | Marshal a value to a YAML string |
| `fromYAML` | Parse a YAML string into a map |
| `indent <n>` | Indent a multiline string by n spaces |
| `trim` | Strip leading/trailing whitespace |
| `join <sep>` | Join a list into a string |
| `split <sep>` | Split a string into a list |

Full function list: [Sprout registry docs](https://docs.atom.codes/sprout/registries/list-of-all-registries)

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://kubensync.com/getting-started/): Installation via kubectl or Helm, prerequisites
- [Usage](https://kubensync.com/usage/): Full ManagedResource spec reference, status conditions
- [Selectors](https://kubensync.com/reference/selectors/): Namespace regex and label selector details
- [Template](https://kubensync.com/reference/template/): How the Go template system works, output format
- [Template Data](https://kubensync.com/reference/template-data/): Loading Secrets, ConfigMaps, and arbitrary Kubernetes resources into template context
- [Template Functions](https://kubensync.com/reference/template-functions/): Available Sprout functions (base64, YAML, string, collections, math)
- [Examples Overview](https://kubensync.com/examples/): Index of real-world use cases

## Examples

- [Propagate a pull secret](https://kubensync.com/examples/pull-secret/): Clone a Secret from one namespace to all dev namespaces
- [RBAC setup](https://kubensync.com/examples/rbac-handling/): Create Role and RoleBinding in matching namespaces
- [Service Account](https://kubensync.com/examples/service-account/): Create a ServiceAccount in every matching namespace
- [Resource Quota](https://kubensync.com/examples/quota/): Apply quotas with create-only mode to avoid overwriting user changes

## ManagedResource spec shape

```yaml
apiVersion: automation.kubensync.com/v1alpha1
kind: ManagedResource
metadata:
  name: example
spec:
  avoidResourceUpdate: false          # if true, never update existing resources
  namespaceSelector:
    regex: "^dev-.*"                  # Go regex on namespace name
    labelSelector:                    # standard Kubernetes label selector
      matchLabels:
        env: dev
  template:
    data:
      - name: pull_secret             # accessed as .Data.pull_secret.<key>
        type: Secret                  # Secret | ConfigMap | KubernetesResource
        ref:
          name: my-secret
          namespace: default
    literal: | # go template string with access to .Namespace and .Data, supports multiple resources separated by --- and functions from the Sprout library
      ---
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Secret
      metadata:
        name: my-secret
        namespace: {{ .Namespace.Name }}
      data:
        token: '{{ index .Data.pull_secret "token" | base64Encode }}'
```

## Optional

- [Versions / Changelog](https://kubensync.com/versions/): Release notes per version
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/eryalito/kubensync-operator): Source code, issues, CRD type definitions
